EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a tree and large tree limbs in Lewes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak surface low pressure system and a sea breeze front produced nearly stationary thunderstorms with very heavy rain across northern Kent County during the afternoon of the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with torrential downpours caused field, poor drainage and small stream flooding in northwestern Kent County in and around Kenton Hundred. Doppler Radar storm total estimates averaged between 3 and 4 inches, most of which fell within one hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with torrential downpours produced some flash flooding in Kent County during the afternoon of the 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with torrential downpours produced poor drainage, field and small stream flash flooding near Felton. U.S. Route 13 was flooded. Storm totals included 4.09 inches in Felton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a lee side trough at the surface, bay breeze fronts and a northwest flow aloft helped produce strong to locally severe thunderstorms during the second half of the afternoon into the first half of the evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down tree limbs and a tree at the intersection of Canterberry Road and Andrews Lake Road.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down tree limbs and a tree in Fenwick Island.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a couple of trees in Dagsboro Hundred.

The combination of another upper air disturbance and a weak surface trough triggered scattered strong to severe thunderstorms during the afternoon of the 19th and then a line of strong to severe thunderstorms during the early evening of the 19th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm tore down a couple of trees in Clayton, one of which damaged a home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Some strong thunderstorms developed across southern Delaware during the afternoon of the 1st preceding the arrival of a cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced three funnel clouds in and around Clarksville (Baltimore Hundred), but they did not touch down and the thunderstorm did not cause any wind or hail related damage.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with torrential downpours caused small stream and field flash flooding in Baltimore Hundred. Doppler Radar storm total estimates reached between 4 and 5 inches, most of which fell within one hour.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down several trees and wires in and around the Newark area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: In addition to the flooding rains, some locally severe thunderstorms moved through central Delaware during the late morning of the 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a couple of trees and wires in Camden.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: In addition to the flooding rains, some locally severe thunderstorms moved through central Delaware during the late morning of the 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a few trees in Dover.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to locally severe thunderstorms that formed near a stationary frontal boundary over the higher terrain of the Virginias and south central Pennsylvania, moved across Delaware overnight on the 11th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter size hail fell just north of Newark during a severe thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper air disturbance coupled with a surface trough helped trigger strong to locally severe thunderstorms during the evening in northern Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm tore down large tree limbs in Hockessin (Mill Creek Hundred).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms, which occurred from the morning of the 18th into the overnight of the 18th caused flash flooding in northern New Castle County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms caused flash flooding in the Talleyville section of Brandywine Township. A couple of vehicles became trapped and a couple of homes were isolated by the flood waters. Doppler Radar storm total estimates reached between 5 and 6 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms preceding a cold front dropped three to eight inches across the northern tier of Kent County and central and southern New Castle County of rain mainly during the first half of the day on the 14th. The greatest concentration of heavier rain was in southern New Castle County. This caused flash flooding of streams and fields as well as poor drainage flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Repeat thunderstorms with heavy rain dropped around six inches of rain across most of the southern third of New Castle County and three to six inches across the remainder of the county. This caused small stream, field and poor drainage flash flooding. A vehicle became stuck in flood waters in Christiana. Sections of Governor Printz Boulevard near Wilmington and Delaware State Route 7 were flooded. Event precipitation totals included 7.67 inches in Middletown, 7.38 inches in Blackbird, 6.32 inches in Glasgow, 5.47 inches in Newark and 3.13 inches at the New Castle County Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper air disturbance coupled with a surface trough helped trigger strong to locally severe thunderstorms during the evening in northern Delaware.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm dropped quarter size hail in Claymont (Brandywine Hundred).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of an unseasonably hot and humid air mass and an approaching cold front helped trigger scattered strong to severe thunderstorms that moved across Delaware during the evening and overnight of the 9th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a few trees in Smyrna.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of an unseasonably hot and humid air mass and an approaching cold front helped trigger scattered strong to severe thunderstorms that moved across Delaware during the evening and overnight of the 9th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a Harrington home in the Farmington area on Staytonsville Road near Wolf���s Trail End Drive. The ensuing fire caused about $200,000 in damage. Four fire companies battled the blaze and the residents were evacuated without injury. One firefighter was injured battling the blaze.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in Newark and caused minor damage to it.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm knocked down a few trees in the Oak Orchard area of Indian River Hundred.
